# Last Time

::: {.incremental}
+ **Core Loop Patterns**
+ **Nested Loops** 
+ **Coding Exercise** 
:::

::: notes
Week 9 second lecture. Students have seen all three loop types and nested loops. This lecture pivots to a new topic area: file I/O.
:::

---

# Today's Agenda

- File I/O 
- Random Access

::: notes

:::

{{< include topic_1_file_io.qmd >}}
{{< include topic_2_sequential_lab.qmd >}}
{{< include topic_3_random_access.qmd >}}

---

# Key Takeaways

- Files persist data beyond the program's lifetime — use `ofstream` to write, `ifstream` to read
- Always check `is_open()` before reading or writing
- Sequential access reads from start to finish; random access jumps directly to any byte offset
- Fixed-size records make random access predictable: offset = `N × sizeof(Record)`

---

# Next Time

---

# Appendix

---

## File I/O: Good Practices

---

## File I/O: Common Mistakes

---

## File I/O: More Examples

---

## File I/O: Practice Problems

---

## File I/O: AI Search Terms

---

## File I/O: Questions for Reflection

---

## Sequential File I/O: Good Practices

---

## Sequential File I/O: Common Mistakes

---

## Sequential File I/O: More Examples

---

## Sequential File I/O: Practice Problems

---

## Sequential File I/O: AI Search Terms

---

## Sequential File I/O: Questions for Reflection

---

## Random Access: Good Practices

---

## Random Access: Common Mistakes

---

## Random Access: More Examples

---

## Random Access: Practice Problems

---

## Random Access: AI Search Terms

---

## Random Access: Questions for Reflection
